text: Debugging is an essential skill for any programmer. When you first identify a bug, try to reproduce it consistently. A systematic approach is key: start by checking the most obvious places, like recent code changes or input values. Debugging tools can be very helpful; for example, a debugger lets you pause your program and inspect variables step by step. Alternatively, logging is a simple but powerful strategy—insert print statements at critical points to track what your code is doing. This way, you can compare actual values with expected ones. By staying patient and methodical, you’ll find bugs much faster.
